title: Phase 2 — Space metrics: computed vs proposed vs blocked
provenance: Salvaged from workflow space-metrics-harden-v1 (run wf_ed3a0ff4-724): 61 agents, ~6.7h, build -> adversarial-panel -> remediate loops. The workflow originally halted on an environmental I/O stall (since RESOLVED, see status) before the cross-cutting package gate ran. The three metric JSONs were computed and adversarially hardened while the PFF data was readable; this ledger is hand-synthesized from those on-disk outputs, which have since been re-verified to reproduce byte-identically off the local copy.

status.workflow_outcome: data-access stall RESOLVED; pipeline reproduces locally. The original mid-run halt was an environmental I/O stall (not a logic error); the three metric outputs survived on local SSD and now re-run cleanly in 7-14s. The only remaining open step is this cross-cutting package gate, which is IN PROGRESS.
status.root_cause_resolved: Original stall: the startup disk was at 100% (0.2 GB free), so macOS offloaded the cloud-synced PFF files (dataless placeholders at the old ~/Desktop/drive-download-... path) and could not re-download into a full disk. Directory listing was instant (metadata local) but reading file CONTENTS blocked ~60min then returned OSError errno 89 (Operation canceled) — real ~3589s / user ~4s CPU. This was an infrastructure/disk-space problem, never a logic problem.
status.per_metric_convergence: occlusion stabilized ~14:20, turn ~14:42, coce ~15:53 (cross-team-contamination fix was its last edit). Per-metric adversarial loops ran extensively; the cross-cutting PACKAGE GATE is the closing step and is running now against the restored local data.
status.data_blocker_status: CLEARED. PFF data is materialized on a stable LOCAL (non-cloud) path at ~/pff_wc22_local; the 3 scripts default PFF_ROOT there and run in 7-14s off local disk with no I/O stall. A one-time parsed parquet cache is still RECOMMENDED (speed/robustness for the next heavy fan-out) but is NOT a blocker for re-runs or the website.
status.resolution: RESOLVED 2026-06-16. Freed ~10 GB (caches + two already-pushed repos), copied the full dataset to ~/pff_wc22_local (patched 2 missing top-level matches 3827/3831 -> 64/64), and repointed the 3 metric scripts' default PFF_ROOT there. RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16 (this package gate): all three scripts re-run in 7-14s off local disk and the regenerated JSONs are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the canonical research/data/*.json. Anchors confirmed: CoCE 518/508, TURN turned-to-goal 0.1822 + Di Maria 6 in final, occlusion 40.9% over 3,180,260 rows. Pipeline is no longer cloud-dependent. Still recommended (not required): a parsed parquet cache before the next heavy multi-agent fan-out.

computed.substrate: PFF event snapshots, all 64 matches (event-snapshot, no tracking cache)
computed.evidence: Field is `visibility` in {VISIBLE, ESTIMATED}, present on 100% of player rows (distinct from a secondary `confidence` HIGH/MED/LOW field). ESTIMATED fraction: overall 40.9% (1,301,065 / 3,180,260 rows); on-ball 16.7%; off-ball 42.0%; attacking off-ball 43.2%; defending 39.8%; ball 31.4%. Worst match 10510. Exports is_estimated()/is_visible()/VISIBILITY_FIELD/ESTIMATED_VALUE plus a gate_bias breakdown by distance-from-ball (10m bins) and lateral |y|.
computed.finding: Prior framework claimed ~46% off-ball imputed; the real measured figure is 40.9% overall / 42.0% off-ball — slightly lower than claimed. On-ball ~83% visible (16.7% estimated) confirms the prior anchor. LOAD-BEARING CROSS-METRIC FINDING: the gate is informatively-missing (NOT MAR) along three compounding axes — distance-from-ball (~12% ESTIMATED near-ball -> ~95% beyond 60m), lateral |y| (central more occluded than wide), and POSITION GROUP (VISIBLE keep-rate GK 11.1% / CF 53.7% / W 57.6% / CM 71.2% / DM 70.7%), and the position bias is PARTLY ORTHOGONAL to distance — it persists within every distance bin (e.g. 30-40m: CM ~55% vs CF ~37% vs GK ~7%), so distance-stratification ALONE does NOT de-bias the gate. This directly bounds CoCE and TURN: their visibility-gated SOFT-xT columns run on forward/winger-dominated boards (En-Nesyri, Di Maria, Kramaric, Gakpo, Mbappe, Gnabry, Ronaldo) and therefore over-sample near-ball central midfielders and UNDER-sample exactly the forwards/wingers the boards highlight, biasing those soft-xT magnitudes downward. The CoCE/TURN HARD COUNTS are unaffected (label-driven, not position-driven); only their soft value reads inherit this bias. The same gate-bias is the chief reason the deferred SAR metric stays blocked.

computed.item: CoCE — Ignored Open Man Index (counts + leaderboards)
computed.substrate: PFF event data (betterOptionPlayerId), all 64 matches; does NOT use the 44-match tracking cache
computed.evidence: 518 betterOptionPlayerId tags -> 8 self-references excluded (betterOptionPlayerId == on-ball actor: analyst flagged a shot/header the player should have taken himself) -> 2 cross-team-contaminated events excluded (gid 3848 Skov Olsen/DEN credited to AUS; gid 3854 Pedri/ESP credited to JPN) -> 508 tallied [anchor hit]. Ignored-Creator (most-ignored open man) leading tier: a SIX-way tie at N=7 net (Youssef En-Nesyri/Morocco, Ferran Torres/Spain, Bruno Fernandes/Portugal, Cristiano Ronaldo/Portugal, David Raum/Germany, Aleksandar Mitrovic/Serbia), every row tie_group=6 with the SAME Poisson 95% CI [4.35, 9.65] — NO separable rank-1 and NO supportable within-tie order (En-Nesyri appears first only by sort/insertion order). En-Nesyri's N=7 is the anchor-confirmed reproduction: his RAW N=8 drops to N=7 net after his single self-reference event, joining the tie — that raw-count provenance is a reproduction check, NOT a #1 finding. See coce.json meta.ROBUSTNESS_DISCLAIMER + meta.self_reference.headline_effect + per-row tie_group/ci_lo/ci_hi. Top Closed Eye (passer-side, by SOFT xT-proxy forgone, concentration-prone): Serge Gnabry (Germany) — directional only. Team ignoring most: Argentina (raw count #1, 32 misses, exposure-driven over 7 games); Germany (per-game #1, 9.667/game). CoCE has only these two team boards — there is NO per-match CoCE board, so no Spain-per-match CoCE figure exists (Spain is 5th per-game at 5.0/game; the '11.0 per-match' figure belongs to TURN, not CoCE).
computed.value_read: SOFT static-Singh-xT difference (xT at ignored man's location minus xT at the actual pass location) = 'xT left on the table'. Explicitly NOT calibrated P(score) and NOT StatsBomb xG. Directional only. OCCLUSION GATE-BIAS APPLIES HERE: the xt_proxy_*_both_visible columns are visibility-gated and survive on only 282/508 events (55.5% both-endpoints VISIBLE; ball ESTIMATED on 36.6%). Per the occlusion audit, the VISIBLE-only gate is informatively-missing by position group (keep-rate GK ~11%, forwards/wingers ~54-58%, central midfielders ~71%) and that bias persists within every distance bin, so the both-visible xT magnitudes systematically UNDER-sample exactly the forwards/wingers (En-Nesyri, Gnabry, Ronaldo, Di Maria) who headline these boards. The both-visible xT reads on forward-heavy rows are therefore downward/selection-biased and must stay labelled directional only.
computed.confidence: high on the HARD counts/leaderboards (CI-disciplined, tie-grouped, anchor-confirmed); the xT-proxy magnitude is directional only and additionally inherits the occlusion position gate-bias on its both-visible columns

computed.substrate: PFF event snapshots, all 64 matches; robust to occlusion (count layer)
computed.evidence: Definition: completed (passOutcomeType=='C'), open-play (setpieceType=='O') pass with PFF linesBrokenType populated AND receiverFacingType=='G', attributed to receiver. 848 TURN receptions tournament-wide. Turned-to-goal share 18.22% [anchor hit]. Geometric overlap with >=3-bypassed line-break corridor 21.44% [anchor hit; honest divergence preserved — TURN measures a different, graded construct]. Soft static-xT at reception ~0.022. Final (PFF 10517): Di Maria 6 [anchor hit], Argentina 17 as a team; Argentina 67 total tournament. Robustness: every count carries an exact Poisson 95% CI; the board is a 36-player 'statistically indistinguishable' leading tier (counts 6-11) with NO supportable single #1 (named cluster: Kramaric 11; six-way tie at 9 incl. Gakpo/Bellingham/Di Maria). Per-match (exposure-adjusted): Spain 11.0, Argentina 9.57. Denominator rebuilt to per-appearance (on-ball involvement), not roster.
computed.value_read: SOFT static-xT only. Deliberately does NOT credit ensuing-possession StatsBomb xG (no PFF<->StatsBomb temporal/possession join exists) and does NOT build a next-receiver phantom-turn term (receiverFacingType exists only on completed passes). The HARD count layer (848 TURN receptions, turned-to-goal share, leaderboards) is occlusion-robust because it is driven purely by PFF human event labels, not imputed positions. The SOFT reads are NOT: the soft-xT read survives on only 710/848 receptions with a VISIBLE receiver position, and the geometric line-break cross-check on 146/681 both-visible. OCCLUSION GATE-BIAS APPLIES HERE TOO: per the occlusion audit, the VISIBLE-only gate is informatively-missing by position group (keep-rate GK ~11%, forwards/wingers ~54-58%, central midfielders ~71%) and persists within every distance bin, so the visibility-gated soft-xT/geometric columns over-sample near-ball central midfielders and under-sample the forwards/wingers (Di Maria, Kramaric, Gakpo, Mbappe, Gnabry) who headline the leading tier. The soft-xT/geometric magnitudes on those forward-heavy rows are downward/selection-biased and must stay labelled directional only.
computed.confidence: high on counts (CI-disciplined, occlusion-robust label layer); the xT/geometric reads are directional only and inherit the occlusion position gate-bias

proposed_deferred.item: SAR — Space Above Replacement (the headline metric)
proposed_deferred.evidence: Blocked on: an in-repo PPCF/pitch-control surface (does not exist); a teammate-aware ghost (the motion-forecast head ignores teammates, uses_teammates=False); a per-teammate value surface (the xt_regression head emits one pooled scalar per frame); a label<->tracking-frame join (does not exist); and a per-frame visibility flag in the cache (absent). Requires the foundational builds below first.

Needs a PPCF pitch-control surface (not yet built). Pressure gate is on the carrier, so gating a spatial surface by one carrier-keyed scalar is a global pass-completion discount, not the spatial repair claimed — design needs rework.

Needs a from-scratch Fernandez-Bornn single-frame influence surface (not in-repo) + the next-receiver head; validate against betterOption (335 in-tracking) + movement/position grades; on-camera-only, 44-match exploratory.
Needs continuous tracking + a P(concede) weighting; degraded by 42% off-ball occlusion on exactly the defenders the hull is built from.

proposed_deferred.item: Ensuing-possession StatsBomb-xG credit for any metric
proposed_deferred.evidence: No PFF<->StatsBomb temporal/possession alignment exists in-repo (only a coarse match-level join). Do not attempt until built.

resolved_now.item: PFF-data re-run (re-verification, package gate, new metrics)
resolved_now.evidence: Data is materialized locally at ~/pff_wc22_local; all 3 scripts re-run in 7-14s off local disk (no I/O stall) and reproduce the canonical JSONs byte-identically. Re-verified during this package gate. The earlier 'de-hydrated / reads block ~60min' condition no longer holds.

open_now.item: Formal 'all adversaries satisfied' closure (cross-cutting package gate)
open_now.evidence: The cross-cutting package gate reached a clean round (round 3: 4/4 gatekeepers satisfied, zero blocking) and is now CERTIFIED — see the certification block below. All three metrics are SOLID on their count/leaderboard layer; website greenlight = true subject to the two website-copy guards. No open items remain on the computed package.

go_no_go.verdict: solid (counts) — pass, pending this package gate's sign-off
go_no_go.reason: Numbers are robust and the gate is reusable; most-stabilized metric. Re-verified to reproduce byte-identically off local disk (40.9% ESTIMATED over 3,180,260 rows; on-ball 16.7% / off-ball 42.0%). No soft-value read here. The only open item was this cross-cutting package gate, which is running now; no data-access blocker remains.

go_no_go.verdict: solid on HARD counts/leaderboards (soft-xT directional only) — pass, pending this package gate's sign-off
go_no_go.reason: Counts/leaderboards heavily hardened (self-reference + cross-team-contamination fixes landed; 518/508 anchor-confirmed and re-verified byte-identical off local disk). Boards are DESCRIPTIVE TALLIES with NO separable rank-1: the Ignored-Creator leader is a six-way tie at N=7 (En-Nesyri, Ferran Torres, Bruno Fernandes, Ronaldo, Raum, Mitrovic, all tie_group=6, ci [4.35,9.65]) and the website must surface tie-grouped tiers (read tie_group/ci_lo/ci_hi), never a single 'most-ignored' name. Team boards are RAW (Argentina, exposure-driven) and PER-GAME (Germany) ONLY — no per-match board exists. xT value is directional only and inherits the occlusion position gate-bias on its both-visible columns (apply the website-copy guard). No data-access blocker remains; the only open item was this package gate.

go_no_go.verdict: solid on HARD counts (soft-xT/geometric directional only) — pass, pending this package gate's sign-off
go_no_go.reason: Counts CI-disciplined and anchor-confirmed (848, turned-to-goal 0.1822, geometric 0.2144, Di Maria 6 in final, 36-player indistinguishable leading tier) and re-verified byte-identical off local disk; ensuing-xG correctly excluded; xT/geometric value directional and gate-biased (apply the website-copy guard). No data-access blocker remains; the only open item was this package gate.

website_readiness: PASS, conditioned on this package gate's sign-off. The data-access blocker is CLEARED (data local at ~/pff_wc22_local, scripts re-run in 7-14s, JSONs reproduce byte-identically). All three metrics are SOLID on their HARD counts/leaderboards; soft-xT/geometric reads stay DIRECTIONAL only (SOFT static-Singh-xT proxy, NOT P(score), NOT StatsBomb xG) and must carry the occlusion gate-bias + tie-discipline website-copy guards below. The website phase may start on these certified outputs once this gate signs off; SAR/P-OBSO/SMS/CHASE and any ensuing-possession xG credit remain PROPOSED/deferred (see proposed_deferred) and are out of scope for that first website build.
from_scratch_recommendations: DONE — PFF data was moved OFF the cloud-synced Desktop to a stable local path (~/pff_wc22_local) and the 3 scripts default $PFF_ROOT there. This was the prerequisite for everything downstream and is satisfied.

Build a one-time parsed event cache (all 64 PFF event JSON -> one parquet/feather). Scripts then read in seconds, immune to both the per-run slowness and the cloud de-hydration that crashed the workflow. This is also what the eventual website needs.

For the next metric tier (SAR et al.), do NOT reuse the stale model heads — the stale-framework auditor lens confirmed the motion-forecast ghost (teammate-blind) and the xt_regression head (pooled scalar) are unfit. Prefer transparent raw-event geometry + a from-scratch PPCF surface + the static Singh xT grid.
Keep all value reads labeled SOFT (xT-proxy) until a real, validated value model exists; never promote them to 'value'/'xG' in website copy.

WEBSITE-COPY GUARD (tie discipline): Any CoCE explainer MUST present the Ignored-Creator and Closed-Eye boards as tie-grouped tiers (read tie_group / ci_lo / ci_hi per row), NEVER as a ranked #1 — there is no separable 'most-ignored open man' (the leading tier is a six-way tie at N=7). Apply the same no-#1 discipline TURN already uses (36-player indistinguishable leading tier). The only clean single-name anchors safe to headline are unambiguous counts like Di Maria 6 in the final. Do NOT borrow a TURN per-match figure (e.g. Spain 11.0/match) for any CoCE team claim — CoCE has no per-match board.

WEBSITE-COPY GUARD (occlusion bound): Whenever a CoCE/TURN SOFT-xT or geometric magnitude is surfaced, carry the occlusion gate-bias caveat — those visibility-gated columns under-sample the forwards/wingers who headline the boards (GK ~11% / CF/W ~54-58% / CM ~71% keep-rate, bias persists within distance bins), so the soft magnitudes on forward-heavy rows are directional/selection-biased, not calibrated. The HARD counts are occlusion-robust and can be shown as-is.

certification.certifier: Phase-2 space-metrics package certifier (cross-cutting gate close-out)
certification.gate_history: 3 gatekeeper rounds. Round 1: 0/4 satisfied (CoCE single-#1 crowning vs the metric's own six-way-tie disclaimer; occlusion gate-bias never wired to the CoCE/TURN soft reads; 'Spain per-match' TURN figure mis-attributed to a non-existent CoCE per-match board). Round 2: 1/4 satisfied (ledger + readiness internally self-contradictory about whether the data blocker was resolved). Round 3: 4/4 satisfied, ZERO blocking objections — clean round reached.
certification.verification: Read-only re-verification off local disk (~/pff_wc22_local) during this gate. Anchors confirmed byte-consistent with canonical JSONs: CoCE total 518 -> 508 tallied (8 self-ref + 2 cross-team excluded); Ignored-Creator leading tier is a SIX-way tie at N=7 (En-Nesyri, Ferran Torres, Bruno Fernandes, Ronaldo, Raum, Mitrovic), all tie_group=6, all ci [4.35,9.65]; CoCE team boards are RAW (Argentina 32) and PER-GAME (Germany 9.667) ONLY — no per-match board, Spain is 5th per-game (5.0). TURN 848 receptions, turned-to-goal 0.1822, geometric overlap 0.2144 (146/681 both-visible), soft-xT 0.02204 (710/848 visible), Di Maria 6 / Argentina 17 in the final (PFF 10517), 36-player indistinguishable leading tier with no single #1. Occlusion 0.4091 ESTIMATED over 3,180,260 rows (on-ball 0.1672 / off-ball 0.42), position keep-rate GK 0.1112 / CF 0.5372 / W 0.5759 / CM 0.7121, bias persists within distance bins.

go_no_go.reason: 100%-populated visibility field; 40.9% ESTIMATED over 3,180,260 rows reproduces exactly; reusable gate exports + position/distance/lateral gate-bias breakdown are correct and load-bearing. No soft-value layer to caveat.

Hard counts/leaderboards correct, reproducible, CI-disciplined and tie-grouped: 518/508 anchor-confirmed, Ignored-Creator is a six-way N=7 tie with no separable rank-1, team boards are raw + per-game only. Soft static-Singh-xT 'xT left on the table' is honestly labelled directional (NOT P(score), NOT xG) and correctly flagged as inheriting the occlusion position gate-bias on its both-visible columns (282/508).

Hard count layer occlusion-robust (PFF-label-driven), CI-disciplined and anchor-confirmed: 848, turned-to-goal 0.1822, geometric 0.2144, Di Maria 6 in final, 36-player indistinguishable leading tier with no single #1. Soft-xT/geometric reads honestly labelled directional; ensuing-possession StatsBomb-xG correctly NOT claimed (no temporal join); occlusion gate-bias correctly flagged on the visibility-gated columns.

certification.website_greenlight_basis: all_adversaries_satisfied AND every metric is solid on its count/leaderboard layer. The interactive website phase MAY start on these three certified outputs, subject to the two binding website-copy guards in from_scratch_recommendations: (1) TIE DISCIPLINE — present CoCE Ignored-Creator/Closed-Eye and the TURN board as tie-grouped tiers (read tie_group/ci_lo/ci_hi), never a single 'most-ignored'/'best' name; the only clean single-name anchor safe to headline is an unambiguous count like Di Maria 6 in the final; do NOT borrow TURN's Spain-11.0/match for any CoCE claim. (2) OCCLUSION BOUND — any surfaced soft-xT/geometric magnitude carries the position gate-bias caveat (forwards/wingers under-sampled vs central mids) and stays labelled SOFT/directional, never P(score) or xG.
certification.out_of_scope_for_first_website: SAR (headline), full P-OBSO, full SMS, CHASE, and any ensuing-possession StatsBomb-xG credit remain PROPOSED/deferred (see proposed_deferred) — blocked on missing foundational builds (PPCF/pitch-control surface, label<->frame join, teammate-aware ghost, PFF<->StatsBomb temporal join), NOT on data access. They are correctly separated and out of scope for the first website build.
certification.soft_value_layer_status: The soft static-Singh-xT proxy value layer (CoCE 'xT left on the table' / TURN soft-xT + geometric) remains FLAGGED as directional only and is NOT certified as a value model — this does not block the website greenlight because the count/leaderboard layer is solid and the guards keep the soft layer labelled.

next_steps: 1. DONE — PFF data is materialized locally at ~/pff_wc22_local and the 3 scripts default PFF_ROOT there.

2. DONE (this gate) — re-ran all 3 scripts off local disk in 7-14s; regenerated JSONs are byte-identical to the canonical research/data/*.json (anchors: CoCE 518/508, TURN 0.1822 + Di Maria 6, occlusion 40.9% / 3,180,260 rows).
3. IN PROGRESS — this cross-cutting package gate (cross-metric coherence, ledger-matches-disk, website-readiness certification) signs off 'all adversaries satisfied'.
4. THEN (separate phase) build the interactive website on the certified-solid outputs, honoring the website-copy guards (tie discipline + occlusion gate-bias caveat on soft reads).
5. RECOMMENDED (not a blocker): build a one-time parsed event-cache parquet before the next heavy multi-agent fan-out for speed and cloud-independence.
